Dump the loaded content as JSON or regenerate the RSS feed without running a full build

Usage:
  nectar export [--config <path>] [--output <path>] [--pretty] [--include-drafts] [--json] <format>

Arguments:
  format              Export format: `json` (Nectar content graph), `ghost-json` (Ghost backup-shaped {db: [{data: {posts, pages, tags, users, posts_tags, posts_authors}}]}), or `rss` (RSS 2.0 XML)

Options:
  -c, --config <path> Config path(s); repeat or comma-separate to deep-merge in order
  -o, --output <path> Path to write the export to. Defaults to stdout. Parent directories are created as needed; existing files are overwritten
  --pretty            Pretty-print JSON output with 2-space indentation (`json` and `ghost-json` only). Default emits compact JSON
  --include-drafts    Include posts and pages with `status: draft` in the export. Off by default so an unintended draft cannot leak through `nectar export`
  -j, --json          No-op here; `export` already emits its own format-specific payload (json/ghost-json/rss). Accepted so the global `--json` flag does not error
  -h, --help          Show help for this command

Environment variables:
  Every flag has an env var fallback (CLI flag > env var > config > default).
  Naming: NECTAR_<COMMAND>_<FLAG> (uppercased, dashes become underscores).
  Example: --config → NECTAR_EXPORT_CONFIG

Examples:
  nectar export json > content.json
  nectar export json --pretty -o snapshot.json
  nectar export ghost-json -o ghost-backup.json
  nectar export rss -o feed.xml
